reframe.named_residual_subop: MIXED, CUDA-graph-capture-dominant: disabling CUDA graphs removes 81% of the per-token flip hazard (84/128 eager vs 21/128 captured), so capture asymmetry (graph-captured M=1 decode vs non-captured M=K+1 verify taking different attention reduction orders) is the DOMINANT contributor, with a residual M=K adaptive split-KV verify reduction (num_splits>1 at served seq-len, which plain BI=1 does not align) -- NOT the GEMM (land #680). #743's offline byte-exactness held because the prompt_logprobs M=1-shaped proxy avoids BOTH the capture asymmetry and the M=K verify shape.
reframe.honest_read: BI=1 batched-verify served spec is NOT 128/128 byte-exact (21/128). PPL is BI-neutral (delta 0.0010%) and the residual is predominantly benign bf16-ULP ties (104/107 onsets at <=2 ULP), BUT it does NOT clear the strict tau=0.3 self-consistency gate: 3/107 confident genuine flips, all at the minimal super-threshold gap (max 0.375 nat = 3 ULP), so the pass is tau-sensitive (0 confident at tau>=0.4). Unlike lawine's publishable-config clean pass (0 confident), the K=6 fire-config served residual keeps a small genuinely-confident tail. BI=1 is necessary but not sufficient for literal served byte-exactness; the num_splits=1 verify fix (land #743; routed to lawine #755) is needed to close even this marginal tail.

ppl.note: absolute PPL is on the loadable full-vocab QAT proxy google/gemma-4-E4B-it-qat-w4a16-ct (NOT the deployed pruned-16k int4_g128_lmhead), so it brackets -- not exactly hits -- 2.019; the load-bearing claim is BI=1 vs BI=0 PPL parity (quality-neutral reduction order, spec-decode does not enter teacher-forced scoring)
